subject: Anthropology
title: Politeness and Face
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Politeness and Face.

hook.text: Could you possibly close the door? means close the door. But the extra words protect everyone's dignity.

buildup.text: Brown and Levinson's politeness theory says speakers protect face — the public image everyone wants to maintain.

discovery.text: Positive politeness shows warmth: You look great! Negative politeness keeps distance: I hate to bother you.

twist.text: Politeness norms vary by culture. What's polite in Japan may seem cold in Brazil. There's no universal etiquette.

climax.text: Politeness is linguistic diplomacy. Every conversation involves managing relationships through careful word choices.

quiz.question: What is face in politeness theory?
options: The public self-image a person wants to maintain
